howItWorks.intro: This rounding calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: If digit to the right ≥ 5, round up
If digit to the right < 5, round down (keep same)
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.question: What is the basic rule for rounding?
faq.answer: Look at the digit one place to the right of where you are rounding. If that digit is 5 or greater, round up. If it is 4 or less, round down (keep the digit the same).

faq.question: How do you round to the nearest ten?
faq.answer: Look at the ones digit. If it is 5 or more, round up to the next ten. If it is 4 or less, round down. For example, 47 rounds to 50 and 43 rounds to 40.

faq.question: How do you round decimals?
faq.answer: The same rule applies. To round to the nearest tenth, look at the hundredths digit. For example, 3.46 rounds to 3.5 (because 6 ≥ 5) and 3.42 rounds to 3.4 (because 2 < 5).

faq.question: Why is rounding useful?
faq.answer: Rounding makes numbers easier to work with for estimation and mental math. It is used in everyday life for prices, measurements, and understanding approximate values.
